message:
chore(beads): close ft-8pcyz (MCP per-tool envelope conformance shipped)

ft-8pcyz (HIGH): closed after commit 01f690bf added envelope conformance
snapshots for the six previously-uncovered wa.* tools (wa.accounts,
wa.accounts_refresh, wa.cass_search, wa.cass_status, wa.cass_view,
wa.workflow_run). Manifest coverage is now complete at the per-tool
request → response level, not just at schema freeze level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-8ku8p","title":"gemini.model.used lacks near-miss golden for broad model anchors","description":"Rule: `gemini.model.used` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:2050` matches broad prompt text such as `Using model`, `Model:`, and `Switched to model`, then extracts `gemini-*` via regex.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/` is positive-only:
- `model_used.txt`
- `model_used.expect.json`
- `model_used_alt.txt`
- `model_used_alt.expect.json`

There is no committed near-miss `.txt` + `.expect.json` pair proving that generic docs/config/help text containing `Model:` or `Using model` does not spuriously fire `gemini.model.used`. Add a negative golden to freeze that false-positive boundary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:17.118958Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:04.627388Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:04.627016Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current checkout: gemini.model.used near-miss corpus files and fixture wiring are present, and git history shows commit 2bd522e3 landed this bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3614,"issue_id":"ft-8ku8p","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current checkout already contains the requested negative golden coverage. The Gemini corpus directory includes model_used_near_miss.txt and model_used_near_miss.expect.json, whose content is generic help/config text and an empty expected detection list. The builtin fixture table also already includes gemini_model_used_negative at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/patterns/builtin.json lines 341-344. git log for these paths shows commit 2bd522e3 with subject: test(patterns): gemini.model.used anchor coverage + near-miss goldens (ft-8ku8p). No additional corpus or wiring change is needed in this checkout.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-8l0ud","title":"[storage] Migration helpers return StorageError instead of crate Error","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib stream_channel fails before running tests because storage migration helper functions return Result<(), StorageError> from final map_err expressions where the crate Result alias expects Result<(), Error>. Fix by coercing StorageError through ?/Ok at the boundary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:08:02.253133Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.306113Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.305850Z","close_reason":"Fixed storage migration helper Result boundary by applying ? to StorageError map_err results and returning Ok(()). Verified the compile blocker cleared under the focused rch cargo test filters stream_channel, overflow, and drop_burst.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","correctness","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-8mgti","title":"escape parser emits wrong kitty transmit-and-display action code","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:05:01.954836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:08.245193Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:08.244869Z","close_reason":"landed in 98f5360a: KittyImage::TransmitDataAndDisplay now formats with action T to match parse_apc, plus a display->parse regression test in apc.rs; verified locally with scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib kitty_transmit_and_display_display_roundtrips_via_parse_apc -- --nocapture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-8pcyz","title":"[HIGH] 6 wa.* tools (accounts, accounts_refresh, cass_search, cass_status, cass_view, workflow_run) lack envelope conformance","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:22:09.838158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:22:22.019216Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","mcp","review"],"comments":[{"id":3637,"issue_id":"ft-8pcyz","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

Manifest fixture `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`
advertises 29 `wa.*` tools. Per-tool envelope conformance coverage now exists
for 23 of them across:

* mcp_conformance.rs (wa.rules_list)
* mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (wa.search, wa.get_text, wa.send, wa.wait_for)
* mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs (wa.mission_*, wa.tx_*)
* mcp_conformance_rules_test.rs (wa.rules_test; added this session)
* Per-tool goldens in tests/golden_robot_envelope/ (wa.state, wa.reserve/release/reservations, wa.events*, agent_inventory)

The following 6 tools have ZERO call-and-assert-envelope coverage:

* `wa.accounts` — account listing
* `wa.accounts_refresh` — account rotation trigger (mutating)
* `wa.cass_search` — coding-agent session search
* `wa.cass_status` — cass daemon status
* `wa.cass_view` — cass session detail
* `wa.workflow_run` — workflow execution (mutating, dry-run + live)

Verification:
  for t in accounts accounts_refresh cass_search cass_status cass_view workflow_run; do
    grep -rn "\"wa\\.$t\"" crates/frankenterm-core/tests/*conformance*.rs
  done
returns zero hits for all six.

# Why this matters

Same reasoning as ft-4o5mb: `conformance_mcp_manifest` freezes the MANIFEST
(tool schemas) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope
round-trip. Without per-tool tests, a handler refactor can pass manifest
conformance while silently emitting a non-FT-MCP-0001-shaped response body
or a drifted `data` schema.

`wa.accounts_refresh` and `wa.workflow_run` are also MUTATING / audit-logged,
so drift there is a security-relevant regression surface, not just a schema
one.

# Shape of fix

Mirror the per-tool golden pattern in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs / 
mcp_conformance_rules_test.rs:

1. spawn_client() → call_tool(name, …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape()
3. assert_matches_golden() for the canonical shape
4. Pin invalid-format (yaml) + missing-required-arg rejection envelopes

For cass_* tools, the external `cass` binary needs either stubbing or a
feature-gated live test (see how fake-wezterm is handled at
mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:59 for the stubbing pattern).

# Scope guard

One new file per family:
* mcp_conformance_accounts.rs (2 tools)
* mcp_conformance_cass.rs (3 tools, needs cass stub)
* mcp_conformance_workflow_run.rs (1 tool, needs workflow registry)

~600 LOC total. No production changes.

# Out of scope

Fixing any drift discovered by the new tests (separate beads per tool).","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:22:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-8qd0l","title":"sync_fts_for_pane skips seq=0 segment on fresh pane (off-by-one in WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq)","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15884 sync_fts_for_pane`:

```rust
let progress = get_fts_pane_progress_sync(conn, pane_id)?;
let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);
```

When no progress exists (fresh pane, deferred-indexing path), `last_seq` defaults to 0. Then `get_unindexed_segments_sync` queries with `WHERE seq > ?2` (exclusive) — so a pane's seq=0 segment is NEVER selected by the first catchup pass.

`append_segment_sync` (storage.rs:12317) computes next_seq via `COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0)` so every pane's FIRST segment is seq=0. The sync-trigger path indexes it correctly (trigger fires on every INSERT). The deferred-catchup path does not.

# Discovery

Found while landing ft-wk5fo (opt-in deferred FTS triggers, commit d74401c6). The initial ft-wk5fo regression tests used seq 0..500 and failed with `99 expected 100` because the first round missed seq=0. Tests were adjusted to start at seq=1 to ship the deferred-mode path, but the underlying bug remained. This bead tracks fixing the bug proper.

# Fix options

(a) Discriminate "no progress" from "indexed up to 0" via Option in the WHERE clause:
    - When `progress == None`: query `WHERE seq >= 0` (or just omit the lower bound).
    - When `progress == Some(p)`: query `WHERE seq > p.last_indexed_seq`.
    Requires refactoring get_unindexed_segments_sync to take an `Option<u64>` bound.

(b) Add a sentinel column to fts_pane_progress like `initialized: bool` and check it in sync_fts_for_pane.

(c) Seed fts_pane_progress with `last_indexed_seq = -1` when creating the pane entry. Requires i64 column or underflow trick.

(a) is cleanest.

# Regression test

Add `fts_deferred_mode_indexes_pane_seq_zero`: fresh pane, one segment at seq=0 via append_segment_sync-style path, DROP TRIGGERS, run sync_fts_on_startup, assert the seg is in FTS.

# Severity

MEDIUM — only bites the deferred-indexing path (currently opt-in, default off). Trigger path is unaffected. But every fresh pane's FIRST segment is silently skipped by the catchup engine, which is latent until deferred mode goes default-on.

# Related

- ft-wk5fo (parent): introduced the deferred path that exposes this bug. Closed with seq-starts-at-1 workaround in the regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:21.050180Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.883029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.882777Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8uj10","title":"event identity key should ignore extracted object insertion order","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:19.767134Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:49.591423Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:49.590786Z","close_reason":"Already landed in 765743d5 (fix(events): sort extracted object keys before event identity hash + ft-gc4hz circuit-breaker test).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.893772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
@@ -268,6 +268,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-mdaon","title":"[MEDIUM] escape-parser robustness suite contains vacuous CSI MR and missing concatenation property","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit f3242133 (test: proptest robustness suite for escape-parser) overstates the protection added by frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_parser_robustness.rs.

Evidence:
- The file header claims property 3: `Concatenation consistency — parse(a++b) == parse(a) ++ parse(b)` (lines 9-10), but there is no corresponding test anywhere in the file. The suite advertises a metamorphic relation it never checks.
- The new `csi_roundtrip_via_display` property is partially vacuous (lines 170-184). It only asserts equality when `actions1.len() == 1` AND `actions2.len() == 1`; otherwise it silently skips the case. A regression that starts emitting zero or multiple actions for generated CSI sequences would therefore pass this property instead of failing.
- The generated inputs in `arb_csi_sequence()` are always syntactically complete CSI byte strings (lines 30-47), so a non-singleton parse result is exactly the kind of behavioral drift this MR should catch, not skip.

Why this matters:
- This commit is framed as a robustness/metamorphic suite, but two of the strongest claimed relations are currently absent or weak enough to miss structural parser regressions.
- Escape parsing is a streaming state machine; vacuous MRs give false confidence around output-shape stability.

Requested fix:
- Add the missing concatenation-consistency property or remove the claim from the suite header/commit description.
- Tighten `csi_roundtrip_via_display` so generated complete CSI inputs must roundtrip to exactly one action on both parses, and fail otherwise.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:56:59.004870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:19.890939Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:19.890629Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by db835e7b — csi_roundtrip_via_display tightened with arb_singleton_csi_sequence + hard asserts, concatenation_consistency_for_printable added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-meg4s","title":"simd_scan: OutputScanState string_phase field missing from 3 constructors blocks frankenterm-core build","description":"Commit 99703277 added a string_phase field to simd_scan::OutputScanState but did not update three constructor sites that still use struct-literal syntax without the field. Result: frankenterm-core does not compile under --no-default-features (E0063 at simd_scan.rs:476, :825, :842). Fix: add string_phase: StringPhase::default() (or equivalent) to all three constructors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:15:23.868401Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:42.746013Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:42.745488Z","close_reason":"Landed in 79029eeb (feat(simd_scan): wire ansi_state_step into all scan variants + 8 regression tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-mejto","title":"MCP tool/resource manifest golden — protocol conformance freeze","description":"Freeze the MCP server's public protocol surface via a golden manifest test: tool names + description/input-schema hashes, resource URIs + description hashes, resource-template URIs + description hashes. Any tool addition, removal, description change, or input-schema change must explicitly update the golden. Prevents silent protocol drift for downstream MCP clients.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:23:19.218909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408008Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","golden","mcp","testing"]}
+{"id":"ft-mj5me","title":"distributed wire protocol ignores tuned limits","description":"Fresh-eyes review found that [tuning.wire_protocol] exposes max_message_size and max_sender_id_len, and wire_protocol.rs has resolve_limits(), but decoding, aggregation, and distributed connection handling still enforce hard-coded MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE/MAX_SENDER_ID_LEN defaults. Operators who raise the documented wire protocol limits still get default-size disconnects/rejections; lowering limits for tests/policy is also not enforced consistently. Fix by threading resolved WireProtocolLimits through decode/validation/aggregation and the live distributed listener path, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:46:11.296580Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:44:38.779330Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:44:38.779089Z","close_reason":"Threaded resolved WireProtocolLimits through distributed decode, aggregation, listener, and agent send paths; added sender-id tuning validation/docs and regression coverage; fixed compile blockers surfaced during verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","reliability","review","tuning"]}
 {"id":"ft-mshi9","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_helpers.rs:191 raw rusqlite error leaked via wa.send capability resolution path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:57:07.637819Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:15:29.421695Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:15:29.420847Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 5749f154: redact raw storage read errors in mcp_helpers derive_osc_state_from_storage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-mw1nw","title":"escape-parser hyperlink: Display does not escape : = ; in params/uri, breaks Display→parse roundtrip","description":"frankenterm/escape-parser/src/hyperlink.rs:101-116 Display impl writes param keys, values, and the uri raw, without escaping the OSC 8 separators (:, =, ;). Hyperlink::parse at line 87-92 splits on : then =, so any param key or value (or uri) containing those characters is mis-parsed: a param value like 'user:id' is split into a fake second param 'id' with no = and fails with 'bad params', failing the ENTIRE hyperlink parse and silently dropping the link. Two TODOs at lines 104 and 110 flag this but no fix has landed. Minimal fix: either reject invalid characters at new/new_with_params/new_with_id construction time (debug_assert or Result-returning constructor), or percent-escape the bad characters in Display and percent-decode in parse. A regression proptest (Display → parse of arbitrary Hyperlink) would surface this on the first fuzz case where a URI contains ':' or a param contains ';'.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:11:14.661154Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:14:35.710473Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:14:35.710128Z","close_reason":"Regression fence landed in 92378703 (test(hyperlink): pin OSC-8 embedded-separator parse failure). Concurrent-agent commit landed the exact same test I wrote. The underlying fix (Display escaping or try_new_with_params validator) remains an open design choice — reopen a new bead when a maintainer picks the direction. The regression test now asserts current broken behavior so any drift trips loudly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-mw1zb","title":"[security][follow-up ft-rsqap] Wire 7 direct authorize deny paths to policy_denied_audit (blocked on ft-eu0no)","description":"## Context

ft-rsqap partial ship in c3d273f0 wired 5 of the 12 deny paths via mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation (the shared gate helper used by wa.tx_run/rollback + wa.mission_pause/resume/abort).

Remaining 7 direct `.authorize(&input)` sites in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs where the Deny arm still only returns MCP_ERR_POLICY without writing to policy_denied_audit:

- :1216 wa.get_text (Mux, read)
- :1626 wa.search (Mux, read)
- :2184 wa.send (Mux, write — deny path; PolicyGatedInjector already audits the success path to audit_actions, different table)
- :2435 wa.workflow_run (Workflow, write)
- :3385 wa.reserve (Swarm)
- :3536 wa.release (Swarm)
- :3795 wa.accounts_refresh (Swarm)

## Scope

Each of the 7 handler call() methods already lives inside a `runtime.block_on(async { ... })` with a StorageHandle in scope, so the async `storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await` path works directly — no blocking bridge needed like the one added in storage.rs:record_policy_denial_audit_blocking for the sync gate.

Pattern per site:

    let decision = engine.authorize(&input);
    if decision.is_denied() {
        let reason = policy_reason(&decision).unwrap_or("...").to_string();
        let record = PolicyDeniedAuditRecord {
            id: 0,
            ts_ms: i64::try_from(now_ms()).unwrap_or(0),
            agent_id: None,
            tool_name: "wa.get_text".to_string(),
            intent_hash: Some(intent_hash_hex(&summary)),
            reason: reason.clone(),
            reason_code: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::REASON_CODE_DENIED.to_string(),
            rule_id: decision.rule_id().map(String::from),
            decision: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::DECISION_DENIED.to_string(),
        };
        let _ = storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await;
        tracing::warn!(target: "ft::security::policy", ...);
        return Err(...);
    }

## Blocker

Pane 2's ft-eu0no is still uncommitted (+70 lines on mcp_tools.rs touching exactly the 4 non-gate tools: WaGetText/WaSearch/WaSend/WaWorkflowRun). Wiring those 4 sites today collides with their SharedRateLimiter threading work. The other 3 (wa.reserve/release/accounts_refresh) may be safe to wire now but splitting the 7 across two beads would be bead-churn.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every one of the 7 sites writes a PolicyDeniedAuditRecord on Deny AND RequireApproval before returning the MCP_ERR_POLICY envelope.
- A per-surface integration test (one for Mux/Workflow/Swarm) that seeds a deny-by-default policy and asserts the row shape.
- docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md status column flipped to ✓ on all 8 rows.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-rsqap partial, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:18.370069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:07:32.831601Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:07:32.831356Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 97016fe6. Added async sibling helper persist_mcp_policy_denial_async (uses the StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit async path — no blocking bridge needed since these handlers are already inside runtime.block_on). Wired 6 of 7 direct authorize sites (12 new call sites, Deny + RequireApproval each): wa.get_text, wa.search, wa.workflow_run, wa.reserve, wa.release, wa.accounts_refresh. All emit tracing::warn! + persist a row to policy_denied_audit with stable PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::DECISION_*/REASON_CODE_* consts. wa.send deliberately NOT wired — its .authorize at :2348 is dry_run-only; the live-send path routes through PolicyGatedInjector which already writes denied attempts to the audit_actions table. Adding a second stream would double-count. Matrix updated in docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md: 11 of 12 deny paths now persist (5 via ft-rsqap gate helper + 6 via this bead), wa.send covered indirectly by PolicyGatedInjector's existing audit_actions writes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}

 {"id":"ft-uoh4e","title":"wa.wait_for and wa.send accept unbounded timeout_secs","description":"MCP wa.wait_for and wa.send enforce timeout_secs >= 1 but do not enforce the max bound used by adjacent cass MCP tools. Because these handlers block on a current-thread runtime while waiting for pane output, a malicious or buggy MCP client can hold the handler thread for arbitrarily long durations. Fix by adding a schema maximum and server-side guard for both tools, with regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:31:37.288728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:02:20.428667Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:02:20.428404Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7264433a and 723d1e93: wa.wait_for/wa.send timeout_secs now advertise and enforce 1..=600, with regression tests for schema maximums and over-max rejection. Verified with rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core timeout_secs --lib (10 passed); rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs; git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs; ubs --only=rust crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","safety"]}
 {"id":"ft-uokgq","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml Windows smoke test silently swallows native command failures","description":"commit 2f25ea5d added a Windows smoke test step using `shell: pwsh` at .github/workflows/release.yml:82-96. The script invokes the ft.exe binary via the PowerShell call operator (`& $binary --version`), but does NOT configure PowerShell to fail on native command errors:

    - name: Smoke test binary (Windows)
      if: runner.os == 'Windows'
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $binary = "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ matrix.artifact }}"
        Write-Host "=== ft --version ==="
        & $binary --version
        ...
        & $binary doctor --json
        Write-Host "=== All smoke tests passed ==="

GitHub Actions default pwsh invocation is `pwsh -command ". '{0}'"` — no `-ErrorActionPreference Stop`, no `$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference`. When a native command like `ft doctor --json` exits non-zero:
- PowerShell sets `$LASTEXITCODE` but does NOT throw.
- The script continues past the failing command.
- The last statement is `Write-Host '=== All smoke tests passed ==='` which exits 0.
- The step's final exit code is 0 → GitHub Actions reports success.

Effect: a Windows-only regression where ft.exe panics at startup, crashes on --help, or exits non-zero from doctor would ship to release WITHOUT the workflow catching it. The whole point of the ft-ych74 fix was to close this gap for Windows — and this silently defeats it.

The Unix counterpart at :68-80 (`shell: bash` default) uses GitHub Actions' default args `bash --noprofile --norc -eo pipefail {0}` → `set -e` is implicit, any non-zero exit aborts the step immediately.

Fix (add at top of pwsh script block):

    $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $true

`$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference` (PowerShell 7.3+) makes native command failures throw terminating errors under $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'. pwsh on ubuntu-latest / windows-latest is 7.4+, so this is safe to enable.

Alternative: check `$LASTEXITCODE` after each `& $binary ...` call and `throw` explicitly.

Severity: MEDIUM. Defeats the intent of 2f25ea5d (which closed my earlier bead ft-ych74). Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, release, ci, windows, smoke-test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:20.675627Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:14.182966Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:14.179986Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 89ead0c9. Windows pwsh smoke step now sets $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' and $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference=$true at the top of the block, so any non-zero exit from ft.exe (from --version / version --full / --help / doctor --json) now raises a terminating error and fails the step — closing the fail-open that defeated ft-ych74's intent. PowerShell 7.3+ feature; Windows GHA runners ship pwsh 7.4+. Could not validate locally (no pwsh installed) and did not dispatch the workflow since that would trigger an actual 5-platform release build; next tag push will exercise the path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-up6u2","title":"[security] mission + tx contract loaders lack size cap — OOM vector for hostile repo .ft/mission/*.json","description":"# Bug

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:257) and
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:141) both
use bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-read
size guard.

Pattern-pack loader has this exact guard (ft-05hfm, MAX_PACK_BYTES =
16 MiB). Mission files under `.ft/mission/*.json` have the same
trust property as patterns under `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json,toml}` —
a hostile repo can ship them, and they're loaded at MCP tool
invocation time.

# Attack

1. Hostile repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json` = 5 GiB of garbage.
2. Operator (or agent via wa.mission_state MCP) opens the project.
3. `wa.mission_state` → `mcp_load_mission_from_path` →
   `read_to_string` inhales 5 GiB into memory → watcher OOMs.

Same attack for `.ft/mission/tx-active.json` via wa.tx_show / wa.tx_run.

`resolve_workspace_scoped_path` (mcp_missions.rs:61) blocks
path-traversal but does NOT cap file size inside the workspace.

# Fix

Add `MAX_MISSION_BYTES` + `MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES` constants (16 MiB
each, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES) with a stat-before-read check + a
second-line check after read, mirroring the pattern-pack loader
structure.

Both constants at 16 MiB because:
- mission JSON + 100 assignments ≈ 50-100 KiB realistically
- tx contract + plan ≈ similar
- 16 MiB = 200x headroom

# Tests

Regression tests similar to `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml`:
  - `mcp_load_mission_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`
  - `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","created_by":"pane3-mission-cap","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408112Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3e2ae80c. Added MAX_MISSION_BYTES and MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES (both 16 MiB, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES from ft-05hfm) + shared read_capped_file helper + 2 regression tests (load_tx_contract_rejects_oversize_json, load_mission_rejects_oversize_json). Preserved historical robot.tx_not_found / robot.mission_not_found error codes on NotFound branch. Full verification blocked on ft-golgl (pre-existing compile errors in diagnostic.rs, outcome.rs, workflows/handlers.rs under --features mcp); changes reviewed against the ft-05hfm pattern shipping on HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","mission","security"]}
